CAT11-049.headline: Vicarious Trauma in Therapists
CAT11-049.subtitle: When bearing witness leaves its own weight
CAT11-049.figureLine: A single seated clay figure leans slightly forward with both hands resting open in its lap, posture attentive yet quietly burdened, conveying the cost of holding others' pain.
CAT11-049.sceneLine: A soft light red veil drifts toward the figure and settles gently across its shoulders like a fine mist, suggesting the unseen weight that listening can carry.

CAT11-050.headline: Trauma-Sensitive Spaces
CAT11-050.subtitle: Designing environments that support healing
CAT11-050.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands at ease within an open, unenclosed space, shoulders relaxed and arms loose, conveying the felt safety of a room that does not crowd it.
CAT11-050.sceneLine: A gentle pale red boundary curves softly around the figure at a generous distance, leaving open air on every side, suggesting a space designed to feel safe rather than confining.

CAT11-051.headline: Collective Trauma
CAT11-051.subtitle: When communities share a wound
CAT11-051.figureLine: Several clay figures stand close together in a loose ring, each turned slightly inward toward the others, postures steady and shoulders almost touching, conveying a shared burden quietly held in common.
CAT11-051.sceneLine: A single soft light red thread loops gently from figure to figure, connecting them without binding, suggesting a wound felt together and carried as one.

CAT11-052.headline: Post-Traumatic Growth
CAT11-052.subtitle: How adversity can lead to positive change
CAT11-052.figureLine: A single clay figure rises slowly from a low kneel toward standing, one hand pressing the ground for support, posture deliberate and hopeful, conveying strength found after hardship.
CAT11-052.sceneLine: A small pale red form unfolds upward from a crack in the dark ground beside the figure, opening into a quiet bloom, suggesting new growth taking root where there had been a break.

CAT11-053.headline: Protective Factors
CAT11-053.subtitle: What builds resilience after trauma
CAT11-053.figureLine: A single standing clay figure holds its ground with feet planted and chest steady, one arm raised softly as if sheltered, conveying durability quietly rebuilt over time.
CAT11-053.sceneLine: Several overlapping pale red arcs layer protectively above and around the figure like a soft canopy, suggesting the many supports that together steady a person after harm.

CAT12-021.headline: Racial Trauma
CAT12-021.subtitle: The psychological impact of racism
CAT12-021.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands tall with shoulders squared and head level, posture dignified and unbowed despite a visible weight pressing down, conveying endurance under sustained harm.
CAT12-021.sceneLine: A heavy soft light red form hovers low above the figure while a steadier pale red line holds firm beneath its feet, suggesting pressure borne with grounded, enduring dignity.

CAT13-021.headline: PTSD
CAT13-021.subtitle: Symptoms, neurobiology, and evidence-based treatment
CAT13-021.figureLine: A single seated clay figure sits with knees drawn up and arms wrapped loosely around them, posture guarded yet beginning to soften, conveying alertness slowly learning to ease.
CAT13-021.sceneLine: A tangled knot of soft light red strands beside the figure loosens at one edge into a single calm pale red line, suggesting a nervous system gradually settling toward steadiness.

CAT13-022.headline: Complex PTSD
CAT13-022.subtitle: Symptoms, causes, and phase-based treatment
CAT13-022.figureLine: A single clay figure sits low and turned slightly away, one hand reaching tentatively outward, posture wary but willing, conveying trust being rebuilt one careful step at a time.
CAT13-022.sceneLine: A long pale red path of soft stepping forms unwinds gently before the figure in distinct, ordered stages, suggesting healing approached patiently in phases.

CAT13-023.headline: Acute Stress Disorder
CAT13-023.subtitle: Early trauma response and prevention
CAT13-023.figureLine: A single clay figure stands very still with arms held close, posture braced and breath held, conveying the body's first guarded response in the hours after something overwhelming.
CAT13-023.sceneLine: A sharp ripple of soft light red spreads outward around the figure but meets a calm pale red ring that gently contains it, suggesting an early shock met and held before it can spread.

CAT13-024.headline: Dissociative Identity Disorder
CAT13-024.subtitle: Understanding how the self can divide
CAT13-024.figureLine: Several faint overlapping clay figures share one shadow and lean gently toward a common center, postures distinct yet quietly aligned, conveying many parts of one self learning to coexist.
CAT13-024.sceneLine: Several translucent pale red outlines drift softly into near-alignment over a single grounded form, suggesting separate parts of one person gradually moving toward wholeness.

CAT13-025.headline: Other Specified Dissociative Disorder
CAT13-025.subtitle: Overview and treatment
CAT13-025.figureLine: A single clay figure stands partly softened at its edges, one hand pressed to its own chest as if reaching for itself, posture searching yet steadying, conveying a sense of self quietly returning.
CAT13-025.sceneLine: A blurred pale red outline beside the figure slowly resolves toward a clearer, single soft light red form, suggesting scattered experience gathering back into one steady whole.

CAT13-027.headline: Dissociation
CAT13-027.subtitle: When a protective response becomes a barrier
CAT13-027.figureLine: A single clay figure stands at a slight remove behind a thin veil, one hand lifting to part it, posture distant but reaching back toward presence, conveying a protective distance gently dissolving.
CAT13-027.sceneLine: A soft light red haze hangs between the figure and the foreground, thinning at the center where a clear pale red opening appears, suggesting a protective fog beginning to lift.

CAT13-028.headline: Trauma in Military Veterans
CAT13-028.subtitle: Trauma and PTSD after service
CAT13-028.figureLine: A single standing clay figure sets down an unseen weight from its shoulders with both hands, posture straightening as it releases the load, conveying a long-carried burden finally easing.
CAT13-028.sceneLine: A heavy soft light red mass rests on the ground at the figure's feet rather than on its back, with a clearer pale red horizon opening ahead, suggesting a burden laid down and a path forward.

CAT13-030.headline: Emerging PTSD Treatments
CAT13-030.subtitle: New approaches beyond traditional therapy
CAT13-030.figureLine: A single standing clay figure turns toward an opening dawn with one hand extended in cautious hope, posture curious and forward-leaning, conveying readiness to try a new way through.
CAT13-030.sceneLine: Several fresh pale red doorways of soft light glow at varying distances around the figure, each ajar, suggesting new paths to healing opening beyond the familiar ones.

CAT14-003.headline: EMDR Therapy
CAT14-003.subtitle: How eye movement processing heals trauma
CAT14-003.figureLine: A single seated clay figure sits calm and centered, head turned gently to follow a slow motion passing across its view, posture settled and absorbed, conveying steady guided processing.
CAT14-003.sceneLine: A soft light red point of light glides smoothly back and forth in a slow horizontal arc before the figure, leaving a calm pale red trail, suggesting gentle rhythmic processing of difficult memory.

CAT19-038.headline: Military Service
CAT19-038.subtitle: The psychological impact on veterans
CAT19-038.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands quietly between a steady stance and a softening one, one hand at its chest, posture honoring what it has carried while beginning to rest, conveying duty and its quiet aftermath.
CAT19-038.sceneLine: A long pale red line runs from a tightly ordered formation behind the figure into an open, looser field of soft light red ahead, suggesting the passage from service into life beyond it.

CAT20-035.headline: Medical Trauma
CAT20-035.subtitle: When healthcare experiences leave scars
CAT20-035.figureLine: A single seated clay figure rests with one hand laid protectively over the other, posture quietly self-soothing and slowly trusting again, conveying care being relearned after it once frightened.
CAT20-035.sceneLine: A faint pale red seam runs along the ground beside the figure, mended and held together by soft light red threads, suggesting a wound from care itself tended and closing.

CAT20-036.headline: Post-ICU PTSD
CAT20-036.subtitle: ICU delirium and a hidden epidemic
CAT20-036.figureLine: A single clay figure slowly rises to sit upright from a reclined rest, one hand reaching toward steady ground, posture disoriented but reorienting, conveying a return to clear presence after a confusing time.
CAT20-036.sceneLine: A swirl of disordered soft light red mist around the figure settles into one calm, level pale red line at the horizon, suggesting confusion clearing into steady recovery.
